_meta.purpose: Distinguish experimentally proven conclusions from working hypotheses. Prevents over-claiming in downstream documentation.

evidence_types.direct_file: conclusion supported by server result files; numeric values verifiable
evidence_types.multi_run_pattern: conclusion supported by consistent pattern across >= 2 independent runs
evidence_types.single_run: conclusion from one run only; more evidence needed
evidence_types.inferred: logical inference from observations; not directly measured

proven.statement: v6 bounded search guarantees zero degradation: final_score >= confirmed_score for all processed objects.
proven.evidence: v6b_full: all 10 objects final >= confirmed. v6b_smoke: all 3 objects final = confirmed. v6_rerender: all 10 objects final >= confirmed.

proven.statement: L_WORLD_EV_UP is an effective action for underexposed_global diagnosis.
proven.evidence: v6_rerender obj_002: +0.012. v6b_full obj_002: +0.027. Both exceed improve_eps=0.01.
proven.caveat: Only tested on one object (obj_002). Generalizability to other underexposed objects is unknown.

proven.statement: L_KEY_UP is not effective for underexposed_global diagnosis on this dataset.
proven.evidence: v6_full obj_002: L_KEY_UP delta=-0.0027. v6_rerender obj_002: L_KEY_UP delta=-0.0031.
proven.caveat: Only tested on one object. May be dataset-specific.

proven.statement: L_RIM_UP does not reliably improve flat_no_rim objects on this 10-object dataset.
proven.evidence: v6_rerender: obj_004=-0.0083, obj_006=-0.0007, obj_007=+0.0057(below eps), obj_008=-0.0094. Net: 3/4 negative, 1/4 near-zero positive. v6_full: obj_007=+0.0107 but inconsistent with rerender.
proven.caveat: obj_007's v6_full result (+0.0107) suggests L_RIM_UP may occasionally help. The rerender experiment contradicted this. VLM noise cannot be ruled out as the cause of the v6_full positive result.
proven.conclusion_strength: MEDIUM — clear pattern of harm on 3/4 objects, one borderline case

proven.statement: No lighting action in the current action space exceeds improve_eps=0.01 for flat_no_rim objects across both L_RIM_UP and v6b replacement actions (L_FILL_DOWN, L_KEY_SHADOW_SOFT).
proven.evidence: v6b_full: obj_004/006/007/008 all exhausted budget with max_delta < 0.01. v6b_smoke: same 3 objects same result.
proven.caveat: Only 2 actions tested per object per run (low_budget=2). Other actions not yet tried on flat_no_rim objects.

proven.statement: compound actions (e.g., L_RIM_FILL_UP = L_RIM_UP + L_FILL_UP) are not reliably better than their component atomic actions for flat_no_rim objects.
proven.evidence: v6_rerender: L_RIM_FILL_UP on obj_004=-0.0083, obj_007=+0.0002, obj_008=-0.0094. No improvement over L_RIM_UP alone.
proven.note: v4/v4b/v5 earlier also showed compound actions causing systemic score drops.

proven.statement: VLM stability confirmation (p0+p1 double probe) is necessary to prevent false-positive acceptance of VLM noise as score improvement.
proven.evidence: v6_full obj_007 showed L_RIM_UP delta=+0.0107 (accepted_after_try), but v6_rerender same object same action showed only +0.0057 (below eps). The v6_full acceptance was likely a false positive due to VLM noise between single-probe measurement and post-action measurement.
proven.conclusion_strength: MEDIUM — consistent with what we'd expect from noise analysis, but not formally measured

hypotheses.statement: Mesh geometry quality (surface curvature, face density) is the primary bottleneck preventing 4/10 flat_no_rim objects from reaching VLM score >= 0.68.
hypotheses.evidence_for: VLM diagnosis correctly identifies flat_no_rim. Lighting adjustments cannot fix the symptom. The symptom (absence of rim highlight) is physically consistent with low-curvature mesh surfaces.
hypotheses.evidence_against: No direct mesh quality measurement has been performed. We have not verified that the meshes actually have low curvature. Could also be a UV/material issue.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Measure surface curvature distribution of obj_004/006/007/008 and compare to obj_009. Correlate with flat_no_rim frequency.

hypotheses.statement: Mesh/material quality (not Blender lighting) is the primary bottleneck for the 4 objects with all-good diagnostics but scores 0.65-0.74 (obj_001/003/005/010).
hypotheses.evidence_for: 4 diagnostics = good means VLM sees no specific lighting/structure/color/physics issue. Yet scores are 0.03-0.12 below the preserve_threshold. This pattern is consistent with a quality gap that isn't diagnosable by the current diagnostic set.
hypotheses.evidence_against: The diagnostic set (4 fields) may be incomplete. There could be other addressable issues (e.g., background, shadow quality, material reflectance) that aren't captured by current VLM prompt. We have not exhausted the action space for these objects (only 0-1 lighting actions tried).
hypotheses.alternative_hypothesis: The VLM prompt is incomplete and misses actionable issues (e.g., background color mismatch, material specularity). More diagnostic dimensions might reveal fixable problems.

hypotheses.statement: VLM evaluation at single-image level has insufficient discriminability for lighting parameter changes smaller than ~2% in scene brightness.
hypotheses.evidence_for: Most action deltas are in range [-0.01, +0.01]. VLM score span between independent probes is 0.0-0.012. Signal-to-noise ratio for typical lighting actions is near 1.
hypotheses.evidence_against: L_WORLD_EV_UP successfully produced delta=+0.027 (3x above noise). So at least some actions produce detectable signal. The issue may be action effect magnitude rather than VLM sensitivity.
hypotheses.status: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED — requires systematic VLM noise measurement (20x repeated scoring on fixed render)
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Score the same render 20 times to measure VLM variance distribution.

hypotheses.statement: The controller is at its optimization ceiling given current asset quality. Further lighting/material parameter tuning will not yield average improvement > 0.02 on this dataset.
hypotheses.evidence_for: v6b achieved max delta=+0.027 on 1/10 objects. 9/10 objects had delta=0.0. No action space expansion in the current design would help obj_001/003/005/010 which have no diagnostic signal.
hypotheses.evidence_against: Only 2 actions tested per low-zone object. Larger action space or different diagnostics might find improvements. Also: action effectiveness may vary across different meshes — this dataset may not be representative.
hypotheses.status: WORKING ASSUMPTION for the project, but not formally proven

disproven.statement: Compound actions (rim+fill) improve lighting quality for flat scenes.
disproven.disproven_by: v4/v4b/v5 showed systemic score drops. v6_rerender showed L_RIM_FILL_UP no better than L_RIM_UP alone.
disproven.conclusion: Compound actions in current space are net-harmful. Do not reintroduce without new architecture.

disproven.statement: v2 proved that taking no action is the optimal strategy.
disproven.disproven_by: v2 had action-execution bugs. The no-action result was an artifact. v6b showed that at least one action (L_WORLD_EV_UP on obj_002) clearly improves scores.
disproven.conclusion: v2 is a baseline measurement artifact, not a policy result.

disproven.statement: Higher VLM probe scores during optimization imply real render improvement.
disproven.disproven_by: v6_full obj_007 showed L_RIM_UP delta=+0.0107 (accepted), but v6_rerender same object same action showed only +0.0057 (rejected). The v6_full acceptance cannot be trusted.
disproven.conclusion: Single-point VLM measurement is unreliable for action delta estimation. Stability confirmation is required.
